Lake Como is one of those places that somehow feels even more beautiful in real life. 🇮🇹✨ Between historic villas, quiet lakeside villages, mountain views and peaceful boat rides, every corner feels cinematic without even trying. From Bellagio to Varenna, Menaggio to hidden spots like Lierna and Nesso, the atmosphere here is what makes people fall in love with Northern Italy. 🌿🌊 What I love most about Lake Como is that it can feel luxurious and relaxing at the same time. One moment you’re walking through elegant gardens and historic streets, the next you’re simply sitting by the water watching the boats pass slowly across the lake. It’s not just a destination — it’s a whole atmosphere. And honestly, one of the most timeless places to experience in Italy. ☀️ #LakeComo #LagoDiComo #ItalyTravel #NorthernItaly #BeautifulDestinations
The art of static shot. Let the world move. Let the frame stay still. #cinematography #visualstorytelling #cinematicreel #nikonzr #colorgrading

Here is my go to shot list for a cinematic sequence! 🎥 Super simple but very effective! Having multiple angles and viewpoints of the same action unfolding is really important and helps to tell a story.  Here is the list broken down for ease:  1. THE CLOSE UP: start tight, introduce detail first not context. This creates curiosity and a reverse storytelling effect as the scene unfolds!  2. THE ACTION SHOT: show what’s happening. Focus on the process, movement, or craft because this is where the story feels alive.  3. THE SUBJECT SHOT: who is this about? This shot grounds the story in a person and shows the technique, skill, identity and intention.  4. THE MID SHOT: start opening up the environment. It bridges the subject and space, helping the viewer feel present and in the moment.  5. THE RESULT SHOT: revealing the outcome. This is where the viewer can understand what was being made, how and why it matters.  6. THE WIDE SHOT: End with context. Show the full environment, this completes the visual story.  To really elevate your sequence, if you had the opportunity to interview the person you’re filming, they could do a voice over!  I hope these shots are useful for any beginners, there are lots of other ways to shoot a sequence but this is my go to!  Happy filming!! 🎬  @Sony Alpha @Adobe @Sony @Visit Vietnam

My goal with this video was to not soley rely on a reference, which made it harder. I had to build a vision in my head first, and it came to me in scattered pieces during random moments all month while editing. The next hard part was actually pulling it off technically, and in one segment I had to compromise on the vision because it would’ve taken me more time than it already has. Even though it took me almost a month, going in with no reference, then grinding through the implementation, is why this is my most unique video yet. It brings me closer to having a creative identity that’s mine and not everyone else’s, and I’d encourage you, contrary to popular belief, to rely less on references and more on your own vision. More coming soon! #davinciresolve #editing #travelreel #japantravel #filmmaking
